children.text: The Secretary shall prepare a National Maritime Transportation Security Plan for deterring and responding to a transportation security incident.

children.label: A
children.text: Assignment of duties and responsibilities among Federal departments and agencies and coordination with State and local governmental agencies.

children.label: B
children.text: Identification of security resources.

children.label: C
children.text: Procedures and techniques to be employed in deterring a national transportation security incident.

children.label: i
children.text: Coast Guard maritime security teams established under this chapter; and

children.label: ii
children.text: Federal Maritime Security Coordinators required under this chapter.

children.label: E
children.text: A system of surveillance and notice designed to safeguard against as well as ensure earliest possible notice of a transportation security incident and imminent threats of such a security incident to the appropriate State and Federal agencies.

children.label: F
children.text: Establishment of criteria and procedures to ensure immediate and effective Federal identification of a transportation security incident, or the substantial threat of such a security incident.

children.label: i
children.text: areas for which Area Maritime Transportation Security Plans are required to be prepared under subsection (b); and

children.label: ii
children.text: a Coast Guard official who shall be the Federal Maritime Security Coordinator for each such area.

children.label: H
children.text: A risk-based system for evaluating the potential for violations of security zones designated by the Secretary on the waters subject to the jurisdiction of the United States.

children.label: I
children.text: A recognition of certified systems of intermodal transportation.

children.label: J
children.text: A plan for ensuring that the flow of cargo through United States ports is reestablished as efficiently and quickly as possible after a transportation security incident.

children.label: K
children.text: A plan to detect, respond to, and recover from cybersecurity risks that may cause transportation security incidents.
The Secretary shall, as the Secretary considers advisable, revise or otherwise amend the National Maritime Transportation Security Plan.

Actions by Federal agencies to deter and minimize damage from a transportation security incident shall, to the greatest extent possible, be in accordance with the National Maritime Transportation Security Plan.
The Secretary shall inform vessel and facility owners or operators of the provisions in the National Transportation Security Plan that the Secretary considers necessary for security purposes.

children.label: A
children.text: submit to the Secretary an Area Maritime Transportation Security Plan for the area; and

children.label: B
children.text: solicit advice from the Area Security Advisory Committee required under this chapter, for the area to assure preplanning of joint deterrence efforts, including appropriate procedures for deterrence of a transportation security incident.

children.label: A
children.text: when implemented in conjunction with the National Maritime Transportation Security Plan, be adequate to deter a transportation security incident in or near the area to the maximum extent practicable;

children.label: B
children.text: describe the area and infrastructure covered by the plan, including the areas of population or special economic, environmental, or national security importance that might be damaged by a transportation security incident;

children.label: C
children.text: describe in detail how the plan is integrated with other Area Maritime Transportation Security Plans, and with facility security plans and vessel security plans under this section;

children.label: D
children.text: include consultation and coordination with the Department of Defense on matters relating to Department of Defense facilities and vessels;

children.label: E
children.text: establish area response and recovery protocols to prepare for, respond to, mitigate against, and recover from a transportation security incident consistent with section 202 of the SAFE Port Act of 2006 () and subsection (a) of this section;6 U.S.C. 942

children.label: F
children.text: include any other information the Secretary requires;

children.label: i
children.text: to identify salvage equipment capable of restoring operational trade capacity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to ensure that the waterways are cleared and the flow of commerce through United States ports is reestablished as efficiently and quickly as possible after a maritime transportation security incident;

children.label: H
children.text: include a plan for detecting, responding to, and recovering from cybersecurity risks that may cause transportation security incidents; and

children.label: I
children.text: be updated at least every 5 years by the Federal Maritime Security Coordinator.
The Secretary shall review and approve Area Maritime Transportation Security Plans and updates under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: the use of public/private partnerships to enforce security within the security zones, shoreside protection alternatives, and the environmental, public safety, and relative effectiveness of such alternatives; and

children.label: B
children.text: technological means of enhancing the security zones of port, territorial waters, and waterways of the United States.

Within 6 months after the prescription of interim final regulations on vessel and facility security plans, an owner or operator of a vessel or facility described in paragraph (2) shall prepare and submit to the Secretary a security plan for the vessel or facility, for deterring a transportation security incident to the maximum extent practicable.

children.label: A
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (B), are vessels and facilities that the Secretary believes may be involved in a transportation security incident; and

children.label: B
children.text: do not include any vessel or facility owned or operated by the Department of Defense.

children.label: A
children.text: be consistent with the requirements of the National Maritime Transportation Security Plan and Area Maritime Transportation Security Plans;

children.label: B
children.text: identify the qualified individual having full authority to implement security actions, and require immediate communications between that individual and the appropriate Federal official and the persons providing personnel and equipment pursuant to subparagraph (C);

children.label: i
children.text: establishing and maintaining physical security, passenger and cargo security, and personnel security;

children.label: ii
children.text: establishing and controlling access to secure areas of the vessel or facility, including access by persons engaged in the surface transportation of intermodal containers in or out of a port facility;

children.label: iii
children.text: procedural security policies;

children.label: iv
children.text: communications systems;

children.label: v
children.text: detecting, responding to, and recovering from cybersecurity risks that may cause transportation security incidents; and

children.label: vi
children.text: other security systems;

children.label: D
children.text: identify, and ensure by contract or other means approved by the Secretary, the availability of security measures necessary to deter to the maximum extent practicable a transportation security incident or a substantial threat of such a security incident;

children.label: E
children.text: describe the training, periodic unannounced drills, and security actions of persons on the vessel or at the facility, to be carried out under the plan to deter to the maximum extent practicable a transportation security incident, or a substantial threat of such a security incident;

children.label: F
children.text: provide a strategy and timeline for conducting training and periodic unannounced drills;

children.label: G
children.text: be updated at least every 5 years;

children.label: H
children.text: be resubmitted for approval of each change to the vessel or facility that may substantially affect the security of the vessel or facility; and

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a security plan for a facility, be resubmitted for approval of each change in the ownership or operator of the facility that may substantially affect the security of the facility.

children.label: A
children.text: promptly review each such plan or update;

children.label: B
children.text: require amendments to any plan or update that does not meet the requirements of this subsection;

children.label: C
children.text: approve any plan or update that meets the requirements of this subsection; and

children.label: D
children.text: subject to the availability of appropriations, periodically, but not less than one time per year, conduct a risk-based, no notice facility inspection to verify the effectiveness of each such facility security plan or update.

children.label: A
children.text: the plan has been approved by the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: the vessel or facility is operating in compliance with the plan.

Notwithstanding paragraph (5), the Secretary may authorize a vessel or facility to operate without a security plan approved under this subsection, until not later than 1 year after the date of the submission to the Secretary of a plan for the vessel or facility, if the owner or operator of the vessel or facility certifies that the owner or operator has ensured by contract or other means approved by the Secretary to deter to the maximum extent practicable a transportation security incident or a substantial threat of such a security incident.

The Secretary shall require each owner or operator of a vessel or facility located within or adjacent to waters subject to the jurisdiction of the United States to implement any necessary interim security measures, including cargo security programs, to deter to the maximum extent practicable a transportation security incident until the security plan for that vessel or facility operator is approved.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary shall require that the qualified individual having full authority to implement security actions for a facility described in paragraph (2) shall be a citizen of the United States.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary may waive the requirement of subparagraph (A) with respect to an individual if the Secretary determines that it is appropriate to do so based on a complete background check of the individual and a review of all terrorist watch lists to ensure that the individual is not identified on any such terrorist watch list.

The Secretary may conduct no-notice exercises in Captain of the Port Zones (as described in part 3 of title 33, Code of Federal Regulations as in effect on the date of enactment of the Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2025 []) involving a facility or vessel required to maintain a security plan under this subsection.Dec. 18, 2025

children.label: A
children.text: facility security plans, vessel security plans, and port vulnerability assessments; and

children.label: B
children.text: other information related to security plans, procedures, or programs for vessels or facilities authorized under this section or sections 70102, 70104, and 70108.

children.label: A
children.text: to conceal a violation of law, inefficiency, or administrative error;

children.label: B
children.text: to prevent embarrassment to a person, organization, or agency;

children.label: C
children.text: to restrain competition; or

children.label: D
children.text: to prevent or delay the release of information that does not require protection in the interest of transportation security, including basic scientific research information not clearly related to transportation security.

children.heading: .—Enforcement of security zones
children.label: 1 .—Enforcement of security zones
children.text: Consistent with other provisions of Federal law, the Coast Guard shall coordinate and be responsible for the enforcement of any Federal security zone established by the Coast Guard around a vessel containing especially hazardous cargo. The Coast Guard shall allocate available resources so as to deter and respond to a transportation security incident, to the maximum extent practicable, and to protect lives or protect property in danger.

children.heading: .—Especially hazardous cargo defined
children.label: 2 .—Especially hazardous cargo defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “especially hazardous cargo” means anhydrous ammonia, ammonium nitrate, chlorine, liquefied natural gas, liquefied petroleum gas, and any other substance, material, or group or class of material, in a particular amount and form that the Secretary determines by regulation poses a significant risk of creating a transportation security incident while being transported in maritime commerce.

children.heading: .—Nondisclosure of Port Security Plans
children.label: f .—Nondisclosure of Port Security Plans
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, information related to security plans, procedures, or programs for passenger vessels or passenger terminals authorized under this chapter is not required to be disclosed to the public.
